note: November at Aihole: 16–30°C, post-monsoon clarity across the Chalukya temple cluster. Early morning light is best for the 120+ rock-cut shrines.
November at Beypore: 24–32°C, Malabar coast dry and breezy. Uru shipyard activity visible along the shore as monsoon ends.
November at Bhavnagar: 18–32°C, Gulf of Cambay breeze cools the city. Clear skies over Takhteshwar Temple make for sunset visits.
November at Bidar: 15–30°C at 700m, Deccan plateau at its most pleasant. Fort walls and Bidriware workshops walkable all day.
November at Chitradurga: 17–30°C, boulder terrain cool enough for the full seven-wall fort climb. Post-monsoon greens still linger between the rocks.
November at Coonoor: 12–22°C at 1,850m, Nilgiri tea slopes at their greenest after the rains. Toy train runs on clear track.
November at Daman: 22–32°C, post-monsoon Arabian Sea calm. Portuguese fort walls dry, beach weather settling in for the season.
November at Diu: 22–32°C, island breeze and clear skies. Portuguese fort and beaches open fully as tourist season begins.
November at Dwarka: 20–32°C, Arabian Sea breeze cools the Char Dham pilgrimage city. Dry season makes temple darshan comfortable all day.
November at Gandhinagar: 17–32°C, tree-lined boulevards at their coolest. Akshardham and Adalaj Stepwell walkable without the summer heat.
November at Gandikota: 18–30°C at the Pennar gorge. Clear nights make canyon-edge camping the seasonal draw.
November at Gulbarga: 16–30°C on the Deccan, Bahmani fort and Sufi dargah comfortable on foot. Dry dust settled after the rains.
November at Guruvayur: 23–32°C in Kerala's temple belt. Post-monsoon skies make the temple elephant processions easier to watch.
November at Hogenakkal: 22–32°C, Kaveri still running strong after the monsoon. Coracle rides through the falls at their most scenic.
November at Idukki: 16–28°C at 1,000m, cardamom hills in full post-monsoon bloom. Reservoir full, wildlife active in the surrounding forest.
November at Kannur: 24–32°C on the Malabar coast. Theyyam ritual season approaches, beaches and Portuguese forts fully dry.
November at Karwar: 23–32°C, Arabian Sea calm after the rains. Devbagh Island boats running again as the coastal season opens.
November at Kashid: 22–32°C on the Konkan coast. White sand firm again after monsoon, Mumbai weekend crowds return.
November at Kochi: 24–32°C in Fort Kochi. Biennale season approaches, Chinese fishing nets at work as backwater traffic picks up.
November at Kodaikanal: 10–20°C at 2,133m, mist over the star-shaped lake most mornings. Coaker's Walk clear on dry afternoons.
November at Kotagiri: 12–22°C at 1,793m, Nilgiri tea slopes quiet and clean. Viewpoints crystal clear post-monsoon.
November at Kozhikode: 24–32°C on the Malabar coast. Dry air returns, biryani stalls and beach promenade at full swing.
November at Laknavaram: 18–30°C, lake levels high after monsoon. The Kakatiya suspension bridge to the forested islands is at its most scenic.
November at Lothal: 18–32°C across the Indus Valley dock ruins. Dry skies and cool mornings make the archaeological walk comfortable.
November at Mandrem: 22–32°C on North Goa's quieter coast. Yoga retreats reopening, Arabian Sea settling into peak beach form.
November at Mandvi: 18–32°C on the Kachchh coast. Dhow builders back at work on the shore, palace and beach breeze in sync.
November at Mangalore: 23–32°C on the Karnataka coast. Post-monsoon skies clear, Mangalorean food and Western Ghats gateway fully open.
November at Margao: 22–32°C in South Goa. Konkani markets busy, Portuguese mansion quarters walkable before the December rush.
November at Modhera: 16–32°C at the 11th-century Sun Temple. Cool mornings best for seeing the carved sabha mandap before midday glare.
November at Munnar: 14–24°C at 1,600m, tea estates deep green after the rains. Mist lifts by mid-morning, trekking routes drying out.
November at Nelliyampathy: 16–26°C at 900m, Palakkad hills quiet post-monsoon. Coffee and tea estates open, birding best at first light.
November at Ooty: 10–22°C at 2,240m, Nilgiri tea slopes and Doddabetta Peak clear. UNESCO toy train running full schedule after the monsoon.
November at Ponmudi: 16–26°C at 1,100m, the 22 hairpins climb through dense green. Butterfly activity still strong across the forest trails.
November at Poovar: 24–32°C at the river-sea-beach junction. Boat-access island fully reopens as Kerala's coastal season begins.
November at Porbandar: 22–32°C, Saurashtra coast cool and dry. Kirti Mandir and the seafront walkable before the December pilgrim influx.
November at Raigad Fort: 16–28°C at 820m, Sahyadri air clean post-monsoon. Ropeway and trek both viable, UNESCO coronation site at its clearest.
November at Rajkot: 16–32°C, Saurashtra cool evenings. Kathiawadi food streets busiest as the dry season gets going.
November at Rani ki Vav: 16–32°C at the UNESCO stepwell. Cool mornings ideal for descending the seven carved stories before the sun hits.
November at Reis Magos: 22–32°C across the Mandovi from Panaji. Restored Portuguese fort walls dry, viewpoints at their post-monsoon clearest.
November at Sabarimala: 15–26°C at 914m, Mandalam pilgrimage season opens mid-month. Forest trek cool but crowded — expect queues.
November at Shirdi: 16–32°C on the Maharashtra plateau. Pilgrim flow is steady, cool mornings ease the temple queue waits.
November at Statue of Unity: 18–32°C over the Narmada Valley. Clear skies make the 182m viewing gallery panorama at its sharpest.
November at Thekkady: 16–26°C at 900m, Periyar Lake full after the monsoon. Elephant sightings common, spice plantations in harvest mode.
November at Trimbakeshwar: 14–28°C at 750m near the Godavari source. Sahyadri air crisp, Jyotirlinga queues manageable on weekdays.
November at Trivandrum: 24–32°C in Kerala's capital. Northeast monsoon tapering, Padmanabhaswamy temple and museum district dry and walkable.
November at Udupi: 23–32°C, coastal Karnataka drying out. Krishna Temple busy, Malpe Beach back to season mode.
November at Vagamon: 14–24°C at 1,100m, pine forests and rolling meadows vivid after the rains. Paragliding takes off as winds stabilise.
November at Velavadar: 16–32°C over the grasslands. Blackbuck herds active at dawn and dusk, migratory harriers arriving at the roost.
November at Wayanad: 16–28°C at 780m, Western Ghats still green. Forest routes open, wildlife sanctuaries at their post-monsoon best.
November at Yercaud: 14–26°C at 1,515m, Eastern Ghats coffee slopes clean post-monsoon. Lake walks and orange groves at their quietest before peak season.
December at Agatti: 24–30°C, lagoon flat and clear for snorkelling. Entry permits required — book the Lakshadweep flight slot early.
December at Aihole: 14–28°C, cool Deccan air over the Chalukya temple cluster. Morning light long and soft, best month for photographing the carvings.
December at Alleppey: 22–30°C, backwaters dry and flat. Peak houseboat season — the 900km canal network books out around Christmas and New Year.
December at Auroville: 20–30°C on the Coromandel coast. Matrimandir grounds pleasant all day, community open for outside visits in the cool season.
December at Bekal: 22–30°C on the north Kerala coast. Fort ramparts dry, Arabian Sea calm — beach-fort combo at its best.
December at Beypore: 22–30°C, Malabar coast fully in dry season. Uru shipyard work audible along the beach, evenings cool and breezy.
December at Bhavnagar: 12–28°C, Saurashtra winter at its coolest. Takhteshwar Temple and the Gulf of Cambay viewpoint crisp in the morning light.
December at Bidar: 12–28°C at 700m, Deccan winter ideal for the Bahmani fort circuit. Bidriware workshops open, mornings can be genuinely cold.
December at Champaner-Pavagadh: 14–28°C, climb to the UNESCO ruins cool and clear. Ropeway running, Pavagadh hilltop temple queues steady.
December at Chennai: 22–30°C, Margazhi music season in full swing across the city's sabhas. Northeast monsoon tapering, Marina walkable by evening.
December at Cherai: 22–30°C on the island beach. Dolphin sightings common in the estuary, Chinese fishing nets most photogenic at low tide.
December at Chettinad: 18–30°C, cool enough to walk the mansion circuit by day. Peak heritage-stay season in the palatial villages.
December at Chidambaram: 22–30°C in the Nataraja temple town. Margazhi month brings special abhishekam rituals — the sabhas fill up nightly.
December at Daman: 18–30°C, peak beach weather on the Portuguese coast. Colonial forts and seafront churches at their coolest.
December at Deomali: 6–22°C at 1,672m, Odisha's highest peak genuinely cold at dawn. Clear skies make the summit push worth the early start.
December at Dhanushkodi: 22–30°C at India's southeastern tip. Sea winds steady, cyclone-ruin walks comfortable before the midday glare.
December at Dholavira: 8–26°C in the Rann salt desert. Cold nights, cool days — the UNESCO Harappan site walkable end to end.
December at Dwarka: 14–28°C, Arabian Sea breeze and cool mornings. Peak Char Dham pilgrim flow — temple darshan queues longest in December.
December at Gandhinagar: 10–28°C, Gujarat winter at its best. Akshardham grounds and Adalaj Stepwell comfortable for full-day walking.
December at Mount Girnar: 12–26°C, cool air for the 10,000-step climb. Ropeway cuts the pilgrimage short, summits clear all morning.
December at Guruvayur: 22–30°C in the Krishna temple town. Temple festival calendar dense this month — elephant processions through cool mornings.
December at Idukki: 14–26°C at 1,000m, cardamom hills at peak harvest. Arch dam viewpoints crystal clear, wildlife active around the reservoir.
December at Junagadh: 12–28°C at Girnar's foot. Cool enough to combine the fort, Buddhist caves and Ashokan edicts in one unhurried day.
December at Kadmat: 24–30°C, lagoon water warm and clear for diving and kayaking. Lakshadweep permit required, boat transfers reliable this month.
December at Kanchipuram: 20–30°C in the silk-and-temple town. Margazhi season brings special temple rituals — early morning darshan is coolest.
December at Kannur: 22–30°C on the Malabar coast. Peak Theyyam ritual season — village temple grounds active through the cool nights.
December at Kanyakumari: 22–30°C at India's southernmost tip. Sunrise and sunset both over the sea — the three-seas confluence at its clearest.
December at Kochi: 22–30°C in Fort Kochi. Biennale season draws in crowds, Chinese fishing nets and cafes busy well into the night.
December at Kollam: 22–30°C on Ashtamudi Lake. Munroe Island canoe trips at their best, the 8-hour cruise to Alleppey books out fast.
December at Kovalam: 22–30°C, Kerala's headline beach at peak season. Lighthouse Beach shacks full, Ayurveda centres booked well into January.
December at Kozhikode: 22–30°C, Malabar at its winter best. Biryani and halwa lanes busy into the evenings as the beach promenade cools off.
December at Kukke Subramanya: 16–28°C in the Western Ghats. Temple pilgrim flow steady, Kumara Parvatha trailhead cool for the early morning start.
December at Kumarakom: 22–30°C on Vembanad Lake. Siberian migratory birds settling in, backwater resorts and houseboats at peak occupancy.
December at Kumbakonam: 20–30°C in the temple town. Margazhi rituals light up the Airavatesvara and city shrines — coolest time for a temple loop.
December at Kumbalangi: 22–30°C in the model ecotourism village. Backwater canoe routes dry, crab-farm and fishing-net village tours easy to arrange.
December at Kuttanad: 22–30°C across the below-sea-level farm belt. Canal traffic heavy, paddy fields dry enough for shore walks between villages.
December at Lothal: 10–28°C at the Indus Valley dock ruins. Cool mornings are the only sensible time for the archaeological walk.
December at Madurai: 20–30°C in the Meenakshi temple city. Margazhi brings the nightly deity procession back to packed corridors.
December at Mahabalipuram: 22–30°C on the Coromandel Coast. UNESCO rock-cut temples and Shore Temple cool enough for full-day exploration.
December at Mandvi: 10–28°C on the Kachchh coast. Palace, beach and the dhow-building yards all walkable from morning through sunset.
December at Marari Beach: 22–30°C on the fishing-village coast. Peak quiet-beach season, fishermen out before dawn on the flat Arabian Sea.
December at Margao: 20–30°C in South Goa. Konkani food markets and Portuguese-mansion lanes at their busiest in the week before Christmas.
December at Marine National Park: 16–28°C on the Jamnagar coast. Low-tide coral walks at their clearest — tide charts drive the day's plan.
December at Modhera Sun Temple: 10–28°C, sabha mandap carvings lit sharply by the low winter sun. Cool enough for a slow full-site walk.
December at Morjim: 22–30°C on Goa's quiet north coast. Olive ridley turtle nesting season — the beach is monitored at night, kept calm.
December at Munnar: 8–22°C at 1,600m, tea estates frost-tipped at dawn. Peak hill-station season — weekends book out well in advance.
December at Murudeshwar: 22–30°C on the Arabian Sea headland. Shiva statue views clear, Netrani Island snorkelling trips running on calm sea days.
December at Nelliyampathy: 14–24°C at 900m in Palakkad. Coffee harvest underway, plantations quiet and cool — early mornings for birding.
December at Pochampally: 14–28°C in the ikat weaving village. Cool enough to spend the afternoon with the looms before the sun gets loud.
December at Point Calimere: 22–30°C, flamingo numbers at their peak on the Ramsar wetland. Blackbuck active at dawn across the dry scrub.
December at Pondicherry: 22–30°C in the French Quarter. Cool sea breeze, peak cafe-and-promenade season — Christmas and New Year busiest.
December at Ponmudi: 14–24°C at 1,100m. 22 hairpins through crisp forest, butterfly species still active on the sunnier trails.
December at Poovar: 22–30°C at the river-sea-beach junction. Boat-only access keeps the island quiet — peak honeymoon and backwater season.
December at Porbandar: 14–28°C on the Saurashtra coast. Kirti Mandir and the seafront at their coolest, pilgrim flow steady through the month.
December at Raigad Fort: 12–24°C at 820m. Sahyadri air cold at dawn — ropeway the easiest start, clear views from the coronation plateau all day.
December at Rajkot: 10–28°C, Saurashtra winter proper. Kathiawadi food streets busy into the night, mornings crisp enough for a jacket.
December at Rameswaram: 22–30°C on the pilgrim island. Pamban Bridge breezy, Char Dham temple corridor comfortable for the long walk.
December at Rani ki Vav: 10–28°C at the UNESCO stepwell. Cold mornings are the only way to see the seven sculpted stories in soft light.
December at Reis Magos: 22–30°C across the Mandovi from Panaji. Restored Portuguese fort museum cool all day, river views at their sharpest.
December at Sabarimala: 14–24°C at 914m, peak Mandalam–Makaravilakku pilgrim season. Forest trek cold at night, queues heaviest of the year.
December at Sakleshpur: 14–26°C at 940m, coffee estates in harvest. Abandoned railway bridges dry underfoot, cool mist on most mornings.
December at Sasan Gir: 10–28°C in lion country. Forest Dept safaris run morning and afternoon — cool light, lions active near waterholes.
December at Srirangam: 20–30°C on the temple island. Margazhi brings the Vaikunta Ekadasi build-up — the 21-gopuram complex at its liveliest.
December at Srirangapatna: 14–28°C on the Kaveri island. Fort walls and Tipu's summer palace cool all day, river walks easy in winter light.
December at Statue of Unity: 12–28°C over the Narmada Valley. Clear winter skies make the 182m viewing gallery panorama at its sharpest.
December at Surat: 14–30°C on the Tapi. Cool evenings pull the street food crowds out — Ghari season and textile markets at full tilt.
December at Thanjavur: 20–30°C at the Chola capital. Margazhi corridors at Brihadeeswara fill by dawn — the no-shadow vimana clearest at noon.
December at Tharangambadi: 22–30°C on the Danish colonial coast. Fort Dansborg ramparts cool, beach quiet before the Pongal week crowds.
December at Thekkady: 14–24°C at 900m. Periyar Lake boat safaris at their most reliable — elephants gather at waterholes in the dry month.
December at Trichy: 20–30°C at the Rock Fort Temple. Cool enough to climb the 83m rock by day, Margazhi rituals fill the temples at dawn.
December at Trimbakeshwar: 10–26°C at 750m, cold at the Godavari source. Jyotirlinga queues manageable on weekdays, Sahyadri air sharp.
December at Trivandrum: 22–30°C in Kerala's capital. Northeast monsoon done, Padmanabhaswamy temple and museum district at peak season.
December at Udupi: 22–30°C on coastal Karnataka. Krishna Temple busy through Paryaya preparations, Malpe Beach boats running on calm sea days.
December at Vadodara: 10–28°C, Gujarat winter in full effect. Laxmi Vilas Palace grounds and garden city walks comfortable from dawn to dusk.
December at Vagamon: 12–22°C at 1,100m. Pine forests cold at night, paragliding windows stable most mornings before the haze rolls in.
December at Varkala: 22–30°C on the cliff beach. Peak season for yoga and Ayurveda stays — cliff-top cafes and Papanasam Beach stay busy.
December at Vedanthangal: 22–30°C, 40,000 waterbirds nesting at the sanctuary tank. Dawn the only serious birding window — 75km from Chennai.
December at Warangal: 14–28°C in Kakatiya country. Fort and Thousand Pillar Temple cool enough for unhurried heritage walks all day.
December at Wayanad: 12–26°C at 780m, Western Ghats cold at dawn. Forest Dept safari slots in demand, caves and spice trails all dry.
